properties.remark: A NWS EMPLOYEE AND STORM CHASERS BOTH REPORTED A BRIEF LANDSPOUT TORNADO TUESDAY AFTERNOON NEAR SHELTON. LANDSPOUT IS A SLANG TERM FOR A TORANDO NOT ASSOCIATED WITH A MESOCYCLONE IN THE PARENT STORM. LANDSPOUTS OCCUR IN A VERY UNSTABLE AND FAVORABLE WIND SHEAR ENVIRONMENT NEAR THE SURFACE. THE LAND SPOUT LASTED AROUND 3 MINUTES OR SO...WITH TOP WINDS LIKELY AROUND 50 TO 60 MPH.
